name: Santa Fe Red
recipeInstructions:
1. Mince 3-4 cloves of garlic.
2. Pour beer, chicken broth, stewed tomatoes, and tomato paste into a large mixing bowl. Mix thoroughly and set aside.
3. In a large cast iron pot, melt 2 tbsp bacon grease.
4. Saute the meat with onions and half of the minced garlic until the meat is well done.
5. Pour the mixture from the bowl into the pot and stir well.
6. Add the remaining garlic, 2 tbsp cumin, pepper flakes, salt, and oregano as it cooks.
7. Bring to a medium boil for 5-8 minutes, then reduce heat to simmer for 1 - 1 1/2 hours, stirring frequently.
8. 5 minutes before removing from the heat, add the remaining cumin and stir well. Adjust consistency with water or masa harina if necessary.
